skill.name: logo-generator
skill.description: Generate AI logos with Replicate, remove backgrounds, and save transparent PNG assets for web projects.
skill.summary: Creates transparent AI-generated logo PNGs using Replicate and local image processing.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: The static critical heuristics are not confirmed as malicious: most external-command, weak-crypto, and environment-file matches are Markdown or string-literal false positives. The script does perform expected network calls to Replicate, reads REPLICATE_API_TOKEN, downloads a model output URL, decodes data URLs, and writes public/assets/logo.png, so publication should include a warning about external services and local file overwrite behavior.

medium_findings.title: External AI Service Receives Prompts and Authenticated Requests
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script requires REPLICATE_API_TOKEN and sends user prompt data to Replicate prediction APIs. This is expected for the skill, but it exposes prompts and generated image workflow data to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly authenticates with Replicate and creates remote predictions using user-supplied prompt data. This confirms external service exposure, but it matches the advertised logo generation function.

medium_findings.title: Remote Model Output URL Downloaded Without Host Validation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script converts the background-removal output into a URL and downloads it with fetch. The URL is model-service output rather than direct user input, but the code does not validate protocol, host, size, or content type before downloading.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of fetch on a dynamically derived URL. The exploitability is lower because the URL comes from Replicate output, not raw user input.

medium_findings.title: Fixed Public Asset File Can Be Overwritten
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script creates public/assets if needed and writes the processed image to public/assets/logo.png. This is intended behavior, but it can overwrite an existing project logo without prompting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is fixed and writeFileSync always writes the processed logo. The impact is limited to one predictable asset path.

low_findings.title: External Command Matches Are Static False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged Markdown backticks, code fences, and TypeScript template literals are not Ruby shell backtick execution. No evidence found for child process execution or shell invocation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation snippets, inline code formatting, or JavaScript template strings. They do not execute external commands.

low_findings.title: Environment File References Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: References to .env, .env.local, and .env.production are setup instructions and error text. The script reads process.env.REPLICATE_API_TOKEN but does not open environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file names appear only in user-facing setup text. The actual credential access is limited to process.env, which is expected for API authentication.

low_findings.title: Base64 Decode Used for Data URL Image Handling
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Buffer.from(base64Data, "base64") decodes image data returned as a data URL. No evidence found that it decodes hidden code, commands, or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The decode happens only after checking for a data URL and stores the result as an image buffer. The surrounding code passes it to sharp for PNG processing.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Model Identifier False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The long hexadecimal strings are Replicate model version identifiers in documentation and API calls. No evidence found for MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged values are used as Replicate model references, not as hashing functions or cryptographic primitives. There is no cryptographic operation at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses REPLICATE_API_TOKEN and sends prediction requests to Replicate. This is required for the skill but should be disclosed to users before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential and API calls are explicit in the script. The pattern is risky because it sends data to an external provider, not because malicious intent is confirmed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Remote Image Fetch
dangerous_patterns.description: The script downloads a URL derived from the model output without checking host, MIME type, or response size.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fetch target is dynamically derived from service output. This is a real network pattern, though not direct user-controlled command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Synchronous Write to Public Assets
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes processed image output to a fixed public path, which can replace an existing logo asset.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write destination is fixed and the script does not check for an existing file. This confirms overwrite risk with limited scope.

content.value_statement: Teams need quick logo drafts that are ready for web use. This skill generates branded concepts and saves transparent PNG assets.
actual_capabilities: Generates 1024 by 1024 logo concepts with a Replicate logo model.
Adds an optional company or brand name to the generation prompt.
Runs a Replicate background-removal model on the generated image.
Trims transparent edges and adds small transparent padding with sharp.
Creates public/assets when needed and saves the final PNG as logo.png.
limitations: Requires a valid REPLICATE_API_TOKEN in the environment.
Sends prompts and image processing requests to Replicate services.
Always writes to public/assets/logo.png and may overwrite that file.
Does not provide trademark checks, brand strategy, or legal clearance.

use_cases.title: Startup Logo Drafts
use_cases.description: Create early visual directions for a new product before hiring a brand designer.

use_cases.title: Web App Placeholder Branding
use_cases.description: Generate a transparent logo asset that can be used in a prototype or demo project.

use_cases.title: Campaign Asset Exploration
use_cases.description: Explore multiple logo styles for a short-term campaign or internal concept review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Logo Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a simple brand mark.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a modern logo for a company named Nova Desk.

prompt_templates.title: Style and Color Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Add clear visual constraints for a more focused result.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a minimal logo for Atlas Pay using navy, white, and a simple geometric symbol.

prompt_templates.title: Product Context Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Include audience and product category for stronger brand fit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a friendly logo for GreenCart, a grocery delivery app for busy families. Use fresh colors and clear shapes.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Brand Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: Specify style, constraints, and asset use for a production-oriented draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a clean SaaS logo for SyncLayer. Use a flat vector style, strong contrast, no mascot, and readable text for a web header.

output_examples.input: Generate a minimal logo for BrayanCodes.
output_examples.output: A transparent PNG logo is generated, processed, trimmed, and saved as public/assets/logo.png.

output_examples.input: Create a vintage badge logo for Summit Roasters using warm colors.
output_examples.output: The skill sends the prompt to Replicate, removes the background, and returns a ready-to-use logo file path.

output_examples.input: Design a simple mark for an analytics dashboard named SignalGrid.
output_examples.output: The result is a square transparent PNG suitable for a web app header or asset folder.

best_practices: Use specific style, color, and audience details in the prompt.
Back up any existing public/assets/logo.png before running the script.
Review generated logos for trademark conflicts and brand fit before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not include private customer data or secrets in logo prompts.
Do not assume generated text inside logos will be perfectly readable.
Do not treat generated logos as legally cleared brand identities.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires REPLICATE_API_TOKEN in the environment before the script can run.

faq.question: Where does the logo get saved?
faq.answer: The processed transparent PNG is saved to public/assets/logo.png.

faq.question: Does it remove the background automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses a Replicate background-removal model after logo generation.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready brand identities?
faq.answer: It can generate logo drafts, but users should still review quality, originality, and legal risk.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. It depends on Replicate network API calls for generation and background removal.

faq.question: Is it safe to run in an existing project?
faq.answer: Use care. The script writes to public/assets/logo.png and may replace an existing file.
